parts.prompt:
Identify ONE method Europeans used to expand their empires in the Americas in the period circa
1450–1750.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Europeans used gunpowder weapons to conquer new territories in the Americas.
1 pt: The Spanish used the encomienda system to expand the areas in their empires under cultivation.
1 pt: Europeans used Christianity to help consolidate and justify their rule.

parts.prompt:
Explain ONE way European colonialism affected Indigenous peoples in the Americas in the period
circa 1450–1750.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: Indigenous communities experienced multiple waves of diseases, epidemics, or even demographic collapse.
1 pt: Many Indigenous peoples adopted European and/or African cultural practices that formed new syncretic belief systems.
1 pt: European colonial authorities used priests and missionaries to convert Indigenous people to Christianity.
1 pt: The casta system resulted in a new social hierarchy involving Indigenous and mixed- race families.
1 pt: Many Indigenous people were enslaved or forced to work in mines or on European- owned haciendas.

parts.prompt:
Explain ONE way European interactions with non-European peoples in the Americas contributed to
the development of a global economy in the period circa 1450–1750.
rubric_criteria: 1 pt: European interactions with Indigenous peoples in the Americas led to the Columbian Exchange, which led to the spread of crops, technologies, goods, and diseases between the two hemispheres.
1 pt: The Trans-Atlantic slave trade brought millions of enslaved Africans to the Americas and significantly expanded the Atlantic economy through plantations and cash crops.

1 pt: Silver mined in the Americas using Indigenous labor fueled the purchase of Asian goods by Europeans, especially after the establishment of trans-Pacific maritime trade from the Americas to East Asia.
1 pt: Economic exchanges in the North Atlantic, including the fur trade and commercial fishing, also connected the Americas to Afro-Eurasia in new ways.
